channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs executive Bobby Motaung disputes R4.3m debt claim
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs football manager Bobby Motaung is contesting efforts to have his assets seized over an alleged R4.3 million debt linked to a property and petrol station he purchased more than 15 years a...

item.title: Manchester United and Aston Villa Face Squad Challenges Ahead of Sunday EPL Clash
item.description: Manchester United and Aston Villa are set for a crucial Premier League showdown at Old Trafford on Sunday evening, both teams locked in the battle for Champions League qualification next season. Manch...

item.title: Exploring How Power Operates and Persists in Australian Politics
item.description: Jo Tarnawsky, a former diplomat and chief of staff to Deputy Prime Minister Richard Marles, delves into the mechanisms of power in Australian politics through her latest series. On the 'Cut Through' p...

item.title: Official Lotto Results for March 15, 2026 from PCSO
item.description: ABS-CBN News reported the official lotto results for March 15, 2026. The article serves as a public information update for all Filipinos who participate in the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (P...

item.title: Colter Wall Cancels Remaining 2026 Tour Dates and Announces Indefinite Hiatus from Live Performances Due to Mental Health Struggles
item.description: Canadian country singer-songwriter Colter Wall, aged 30, has canceled the remaining dates of his 2026 tour in support of his fifth studio album, *Memories and Empties*, released in November 2025. In a...

item.title: Federal Government Declares Eid ul Fitr 2026 Public Holidays on March 20 and 21
item.description: Islamabad: The federal government of Pakistan has officially declared public holidays for Eid ul Fitr 2026, according to a notification from the Cabinet Division. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announc...

item.title: Lagos Records 173 Emergencies in First Two Months of 2026, Road Accidents Top List
item.description: For January and February 2026, LASEMA, wey dey handle emergency matters for Lagos, don report total of 173 emergency cases across di state. January carry 93 cases while February get 80. Road traffic w...

item.title: PM Shehbaz orders strategy for exporting surplus food to Gulf while ensuring local supply
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has instructed government officials to prepare a detailed plan for exporting Pakistan's surplus food items to Gulf countries, while making sure that domestic needs are fu...

item.title: VP Sara Leaves House Waiting for Response to Impeachment Case
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte announced that the House of Representatives will need to wait until the deadline to see if she will respond to the impeachment complaint filed against her. The 10-day windo...

item.title: Two fatalities confirmed in Centretown apartment fire on Somerset Street West
item.description: Ottawa paramedics and emergency services responded to a fatal fire at an apartment building in the Centretown neighbourhood on Saturday, March 15, 2026. Two individuals tragically lost their lives as ...

item.title: Government Confirms Stable Rice Supply for 'Benteng Bigas' Program
item.description: The Department of Agriculture (DA) and Food Terminal Inc. (FTI) reassured the public that the government’s P20-per-kilo rice initiative, 'Benteng Bigas, Meron Na!', maintains ample supply despite conc...

item.title: Prince Harry Addresses Claims About Queen Camilla's Remarks on His Relationship with Meghan Markle
item.description: Prince Harry has responded after a new book by author Tom Bower suggested that Queen Camilla told a friend she believed Harry had been 'brainwashed' by Meghan Markle. The excerpts from Bower’s book, s...

item.title: U.S. Army Signs 10-Year $20 Billion Contract with Anduril for Defense Technology Solutions
item.description: The U.S. Army has finalized a major 10-year contract with defense technology company Anduril, potentially valued at up to $20 billion. The agreement begins with a five-year base period, with an option...

item.title: Gulf States Respond to Escalating Missile and Drone Attacks Amid Iran-U.S.-Israel Conflict
item.description: Gulf Arab states, including Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates, reported new missile and drone attacks as the Iran-U.S.-Israel war enters its third week. The conflict began when Israe...

item.title: Teen sprinter Gout Gout claims open-age 200m title at Queensland championships
item.description: Teen Australian sprint sensation Gout Gout has claimed the open-age 200-metre title at the Queensland athletics championships held in Brisbane. In a thrilling race, Gout Gout fended off a determined c...

item.title: Champion racehorse accidentally served as meat in municipal soup kitchen in Turkey
item.description: A four-year-old champion racehorse named Smart Latch, retired due to an injury, was accidentally slaughtered and served at a municipal soup kitchen in the Mersin province of southern Turkey. The incid...

item.title: Football-linked trafficking of Ghanaian youth to Nigeria: challenges and prevention
item.description: In West Africa, football isn’t just a sport; it represents hope, social mobility, and a way out of poverty. Traffickers are taking advantage of these dreams, particularly among young Ghanaian men, by ...

item.title: District assures uninterrupted LPG supply and doorstep delivery
item.description: The Deputy Commissioner of Mysuru, Shreerupa, has confirmed that domestic LPG cooking gas cylinders are well-stocked in the district and are being supplied to households without any interruptions. The...

item.title: Parliamentary Committee Continues Investigation into Police Misconduct Allegations
item.description: On Friday, the parliamentary ad hoc committee continued its investigation into allegations of corruption and criminal infiltration within the South African Police Service (SAPS). Earlier this week, re...

item.title: Election Commission schedules West Bengal assembly polls in two phases for convenience and security
item.description: The Election Commission of India has announced that the West Bengal assembly elections will be conducted in two phases on April 23 and April 29, 2026, instead of the earlier eight-phase polling system...
